title: Memorandum on Compensation for Transportation Security Administration Personnel During Department of Homeland Security Funding Lapse
summary: A presidential memorandum directing the Secretary of Homeland Security and the Director of the Office of Management and Budget to address compensation for Transportation Security Administration officers and employees during a period when the Department of Homeland Security is operating without an enacted budget. The memorandum addresses operational concerns related to extended funding lapses and directs action to ensure TSA personnel receive compensation during the lapse period.

title: Executive Order Modifying Delegations of Presidential Authority Under the Defense Production Act
summary: This executive order amends Executive Order 13603 from March 16, 2012, which addressed National Defense Resources Preparedness. The order modifies certain delegations of presidential authority that were previously granted under the Defense Production Act of 1950. The Defense Production Act authorizes the President to require priority performance of contracts deemed necessary for national defense and to allocate materials and facilities for national defense purposes. This order adjusts how specific powers and responsibilities under this act are distributed among federal agencies and officials.

title: Executive Order on Substantiation Requirements for Made in America Product Claims
summary: This executive order establishes requirements for the substantiation and accuracy of products advertised as Made in America. The order asserts that consumers have a right to clear, accurate, and accessible information about whether products claiming to be made in the United States are actually manufactured domestically. The order directs federal agencies to develop and enforce standards for companies making Made in America claims on their products, with the stated intent of protecting consumers from potentially misleading advertising regarding the domestic origin of goods.

title: Executive Order on Reducing Regulatory Requirements for Residential Housing Construction and Renovation
summary: This executive order directs federal agencies to identify and remove regulatory requirements that the administration characterizes as unnecessary and that may impede the construction of new residential housing and renovation of existing homes. The order states that its purpose is to support homeownership by facilitating a housing market with increased inventory and construction activity. The executive order establishes a framework for federal agencies to review and streamline regulations affecting residential housing development.
